mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 3.5% year-over-year, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy that suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced corporate profit margins. This dual constraint produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 5.33% and CPIAUCSL at 313.049 index level
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both trigger conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month rolling window signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following unemployment rate increases of >0.3pp are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% annualized rate while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,200 thousand units, indicating weakening consumer and residential demand that signals an imminent demand-side recession. This dual weakness produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth < 0.17% AND HOUST < 1200k units
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 703,088M and HOUST at 1,277k units
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) expands at an accelerating rate above 0.5% month-over-month for two consecutive months, signaling robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains that support corporate earnings growth and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth > 0.5% for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 103.1168 index level as of 2024-05-15
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following consecutive INDPRO >0.5% months are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.15), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100,000 jobs per month while the unemployment rate remains stable or rises, indicating a labor market inflection point where wage pressure eases and corporate hiring intentions weaken. This signals reduced earnings growth and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM growth < 100k AND UNRATE >= prior month level
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 158,638k and UNRATE at 4.1%
regime_hint: labor market cooling with hiring deceleration
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 3.0% annualized rate in the most recent quarter, indicating robust aggregate demand and economic expansion that supports equity valuations and corporate earnings. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 22,758.752 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2024-01-15
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in quarters with GDPC1 growth > 3.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate declines by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period while inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows signs of moderation below 3.0% year-over-year, signaling the onset of a monetary easing cycle that reduces discount rates and improves equity risk-reward dynamics. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines > 0.5pp in 3 months AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth < 3.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 5.33% and CPIAUCSL at 313.049
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contracts month-over-month by more than 0.3% while nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth falls below 50,000 jobs per month, indicating synchronized supply and labor market weakness that signals recession risk. This dual deterioration produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM decline > 0.3% AND PAYEMS MoM growth < 50k
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 103.1168 and PAYEMS at 158,638k
regime_hint: synchronized supply-labor contraction with recession signals
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
